svg.title: Guardian AI Platform — Approval Workflows
svg.desc: Tool lifecycle (registration → org approval → gateway deploy → project grant) and Agent lifecycle (author wizard → dual approval → AgentCore deploy).
svg.text: PART 1 — Org Tool Lifecycle: Registration → Approval → Gateway Deploy → Project Grant
approval_status: PENDING → APPROVED · deployment_status: NOT_DEPLOYED → ACTIVE · project_tool_grants: ACTIVE
PART 2 — Agent Lifecycle: Author Wizard → Dual Approval → AgentCore Deploy
agents: id, project_id, org_id, name, system_prompt, model_id, status, authored_via, risk_tier, created_by
agent_approval_requests: id, agent_id, approver_type (business_owner|platform_admin), status, risk_tier, comments
agent_environment_deployments: id, agent_id, environment_id, deployment_status, agent_core_endpoint_id, s3_code_location
status flow: DRAFT → SUBMITTED → APPROVED | REJECTED → deploying → DEPLOYED
▪ Credential refs must be secret names only — never raw values. UI validates against /AKIA|BEGIN|password|token-value/i.
▪ Deployment is blocked until agent.status = APPROVED (enforced in POST /agents/:id/deploy route).
▪ Both business_owner AND platform_admin tasks must resolve to "approved" before the agent flips to APPROVED.
▪ Rejecting either task immediately sets agent.status = REJECTED; further decisions are ignored.
▪ ETD environment_id must match the target environment (enforced in deploy route; no cross-env resource reuse).
▪ No AWS account IDs, role ARNs, model IDs, or gateway IDs are hardcoded — all resolved at runtime from DB config.
▪ API: GET /api/approvals returns tool + agent tasks; taskCategory field distinguishes them.
▪ Tool lifecycle: PENDING → APPROVED (org approval) → ACTIVE (gateway deploy) → project_tool_grants status=ACTIVE (grant).
